architecture.summary: RouteSAE integrates a lightweight router with a shared TopK sparse autoencoder to extract interpretable features from multiple layers of an LLM. The router computes normalized layer-wise weights via sum-pooled multi-layer activations and a learned linear projection, selecting the layer with highest probability. The scaled activation from the selected layer feeds into a shared TopK SAE for sparse disentanglement and reconstruction, unifying feature representations across layers while incurring minimal parameter overhead compared to layer-specific SAE variants.

biblio.title: Route Sparse Autoencoder to Interpret Large Language Models

claims.statement: under the same sparsity constraint of 64, RouteSAE extracts 22.5% more features than baseline SAEs
RouteSAE achieves a 22.3% higher interpretability score
RouteSAE maintains interpretation scores above 4.4 at all sparsity levels, largely unaffected by changes in sparsity
RouteSAE achieves the best performance among all methods, maintaining a lower KL divergence at comparable sparsity levels
RouteSAE extracts over 4,000 interpretable features at a threshold of 15, surpassing all other methods

RouteSAE enables controlled model steering by directly manipulating internal features from the SAE decoder, allowing targeted interventions to influence model output through feature activation clamping
RouteSAE produces a U-shaped weight distribution across layers during both training and inference, rather than concentrating on a small subset of shallow layers
The router employs a simple linear projection, which has limited capabilities; exploring more sophisticated activation aggregation methods and router designs is an important direction for future work
